text: The system shall provide a graphical user interface (GUI) to handle PDF files.
The system shall provide a command-line interface (Console) for PDF manipulation.
The system shall include an Alternate Mix plugin to combine two PDF documents by taking pages alternatively.
The system shall include a Merge/Extract plugin to combine multiple PDF documents or subsections into a single document.
The system shall include a Rotate plugin to rotate pages of selected PDF documents.
The system shall include a Split plugin to divide a PDF document into multiple parts.
The system shall include a Visual Document Composer plugin to drag and drop pages from multiple PDF documents into a new document.
The system shall include a Visual Reorder plugin to rotate, reorder, or delete specific pages from a PDF document.
The system shall allow the user to save and load the working environment to automate recurrent jobs.
The user shall be able to split a PDF document only one at a time.
The system shall require PDF version 1.5 or higher to compress output files.
The [FILENUMBER] prefix variable shall support a maximum of 10 digits.
The system shall support merging whole documents, specific page ranges, or single pages using a comma-separated selection.
The system shall allow the user to export the list of selected files as an XML file.
The Alternate Mix feature shall support only two documents at a time.
The system shall provide an Image Viewer to preview selected PDF pages.
The system shall display log messages categorized as DEBUG/INFO (black), WARNING (blue), and ERROR/FATAL (red).
The system shall allow users to configure language, GUI theme, alert sounds, and log detail levels.
The system shall require a restart to apply changes made in the Settings panel.
The software shall run on any platform with a Java Virtual Machine (JVM) version 1.6 or higher.
The system shall consume a maximum of 254MB of memory by default.
The application shall occupy at least 13.9MB of disk space.
The system shall ensure that input PDF files remain unmodified during operations.
The software shall be licensed under the GNU General Public License (GPL).
